notes: Auto-executed tool call: the agent invokes a tool mid-turn (a shell command), the tool auto-resolves with no user approval, and the turn completes — the canonical arc is ready→working→ready and state NEVER enters waiting. full-lifecycle-toolcall maps to coverage_id auto-executed-tool-call; the top-level scenarios.json verify.has_waiting_state is claudecode-shaped (claudecode gates via a PostToolUse permission hook), but pi has no such hook (toolCall/toolResult parse straight through, no gating), so the pi arc ends in ready, not waiting — the spec asserts the no-waiting invariant instead. Recorded with the HEADLESS pi driver (`pi --print -p <prompt>`): pi writes one JSONL session file per invocation on launch (FilesUnderRoot, non-lazy), runs the single tool-using turn, then exits. The headless turn is brief, so the curated fixture is a SINGLE rollout session (no separately-matchable proc-<PID> pre-session phase — the daemon's first scanner tick already keys the real UUID transcript). The daemon observes that one session born ready, the working flip on first transcript activity, the working→ready settle when the turn completes (pi's stopReason=stop maps to turn_done), then pid_discovered and process_exited at headless exit. The tool call itself is observable in transcript.jsonl as pi toolCall / toolResult message events (parser.go), satisfying the scenario's contains_tool_call signal; the phase DSL has no tool-event form, so the tool call is asserted only via the working span it sustains, not a dedicated phase. turn_end anchors to turn_start (not session_birth) so the matcher picks the post-turn ready, not the birth ready.

text: Session appears in ready within 1s of launch
User prompt requiring tool use → working
Tool auto-resolves and the turn completes → back to ready; state never entered waiting
Headless pi exits after the single turn — process_exited on the session
